title: sports-registry — trait composition
description: Two owned traits — `ReferencesSport` for downstream models that carry `sport_key` / `discipline_key` / `position_key` strings (Team Wave 2b, AthleteEnrollment Wave 3, Coach Wave 3, Event Wave 3, Session Wave 3, Season Wave 3a, AgeGroup Wave 3a); and `HasSportRegistryCatalog` on the Tenant model exposing per-tenant merged-catalog accessors. `ReferencesSport` is intentionally STRING-based, not FK-based — downstream rows carry `sport_key` as a plain string that resolves through SportKeyResolver at read time. This decouples every consumer from schema changes on this module.

owned.description: Mixed into any domain model that carries `sport_key` (Team, AthleteEnrollment, Coach, Event, Session, Season, AgeGroup, Facility.compatible_sport_keys as array). Adds `sport()` accessor (lazy resolves through SportKeyResolver — NOT an FK relation), `resolveInSport(?string $sport_key = null)` query scope filtering `WHERE sport_key = ?`, `hasResolvedSport()` boolean accessor, and a `booted()` hook that validates the sport_key against the resolver at save time (refuses SPORT_KEY_UNRESOLVED 422 when the key doesn't resolve, unless the row is marked historical). The trait uses the `$sportKeyColumn` static property (default 'sport_key') so models with a differently-named column (e.g. Facility might carry `primary_sport_key`) can override. Also supports optional `$disciplineKeyColumn` + `$positionKeyColumn` — when set, the trait extends validation to the full triple.
usedBy: teams::Team (sport_key nullable — Wave 2b; open-age teams may leave it null)
athlete-enrollment::AthleteEnrollment (sport_key required, discipline_key + position_key optional — Wave 3+)
coach::Coach (sport_key required — Wave 3+; coaches specialise in one primary sport)
session::Session (sport_key nullable — Wave 3+; sport-agnostic sessions like general_fitness may leave it null)
season::Season (sport_key nullable — already exists in Season Wave 3a via BelongsToSeason downstream)
age-group::AgeGroup (sport_key nullable — already exists in AgeGroup Wave 3a for sport-specific overrides)
facility::Facility (compatible_sport_keys array<string> nullable — Wave 2b; multi-sport facility declares supported sports)
composition_notes: Order: `BelongsToTenant` must appear BEFORE `ReferencesSport` so the tenant scope resolves first + the resolver's tenant argument is populated correctly.

The `booted()` validation hook is ON by default — every write validates sport_key. Set `bool $validateSportKeyOnSave = false` per-model to opt out (rare — historical migrations that intentionally carry unresolved keys, or benchmark fixtures).
Query scope naming: `resolveInSport(?string)` mirrors `resolveInAgeGroup(?AgeGroup)` from age-group. Consistency across the sports tier.

When a model carries the full triple (sport_key + discipline_key + position_key), the trait's `resolvedSport()` / `resolvedDiscipline()` / `resolvedPosition()` accessors return each level of the resolver output.

owned.description: Mixed into the Tenant model. Adds merged-catalog accessors: `sports()` (hasMany + a platform-scoped hasMany merged into one collection at read time), `platformSports()` (WHERE tenant_id IS NULL — hasMany via a global scope on Sport), `customSports()` (WHERE tenant_id = current tenant), `activeSports()` (all + is_active=true), `resolveSportKey($sport_key)` (delegates to SportKeyResolver::resolve()). Read-only — every write goes through the CustomEntryFactory + Provisioner services. The merged read pattern is unusual — Eloquent hasMany doesn't natively merge tenant_id NULL + tenant_id=current queries, so the trait ships a custom relationship class.
usedBy: tenancy::Tenant (exposes the merged sports catalog to every consumer holding the Tenant model)
composition_notes: The `sports()` relation is a HasManyMerged (custom relation class) — it queries `WHERE tenant_id IS NULL OR tenant_id = current` in a single roundtrip. Downstream consumers use `$tenant->sports` as normal Eloquent-style relationship.
The `resolveSportKey($sport_key)` accessor is a convenience over `app(SportKeyResolver::class)->resolve($sport_key, null, null, $tenant)` — DI-injected callers should reach for the resolver directly.

framework.reason: Sports / Disciplines / Positions all use SoftDeletes for the retention window on custom rows (platform rows never soft-delete).

tenants.reason: Applies the tenant scope + auto-fills on save. Nullable tenant_id semantics: NULL = platform (visible to every tenant via the merged scope); non-null = tenant custom (visible only to owner + platform staff).

audit.reason: Every Sport / Discipline / Position mutation writes to the `audits` table. SportPrefixResolutionFailed rows retained 7 years (dangling-reference signal); other mutations 2 years.

activity.reason: Registry lifecycle events feed the tenant admin's product-facing activity feed.

localization.reason: Sport.name + Sport.description + Discipline.name + Discipline.description + Position.name + Position.description all localize via the JSONB translations column. Platform-seeded rows carry translations for every enabled locale (per SportCatalogSeeder); tenant customs are en-only unless the tenant admin adds translations via the localization module.

sport.notes: Order matters. BelongsToTenant is first — establishes the nullable tenant_id scope. NO BelongsToApplication (application_id forbidden per hierarchy.md §2). NO BelongsToRegion / BelongsToOrganization / BelongsToBranch (all forbidden on registry rows).

discipline.notes: Same trait set as Sport minus Searchable (Disciplines rarely searched independently — they're always browsed via their parent Sport). Plus the sport_id FK column via a `belongsTo('sport')` relation.

position.notes: Same trait set as Discipline. Plus the discipline_id FK column via a `belongsTo('discipline')` relation.
